## We had a pretty frustrating experience
We had a pretty frustrating experience. The service turned out to be much more expensive than we expected, and unfortunately, the team often seemed unsure about how their own pricing worked. We were given information that later turned out to be incomplete or inaccurate, which affected some important decisions on our side. [...] Evaluating 462 reviews, most reviewers were somewhat happy with their experience overall. Many customers are impressed with the product, finding it fast, flexible, and genuinely useful for various projects, offering significant value and creative freedom. The audio quality is often praised, and the platform is generally considered easy to use. Reviewers frequently highlight the excellent and helpf ul customer support, with staff members often going above and beyond to resolve issues and provide valuable assistance. However, some people were dissatisfied with the product's functionality, citing issues like voices not sounding good, sections being cut off or repeated in audiobooks, and problems with voice cloning. results.title: An honest look at ElevenLabs reviews: Is it the right AI voice for you ...
results.content:
A common complaint you'll see in user reviews is frustration with this pricing model. People often say that credits disappear much faster than they expect, especially when using the dubbing feature or higher-quality voices. Another big issue is that unused credits don't roll over. If you have a slow month, you just lose them, which makes it tough for people with inconsistent workloads to feel like they're getting their money's worth.

This is a very different approach from platforms like eesel AI, which offers straightforward pricing based on your overall usage. With eesel AI, you don't have to count every interaction or worry about a massive bill after a busy month.

## Key limitations [...] ### Billing and support concerns

A quick scroll through Trustpilot shows a pattern of complaints about billing problems, the confusing credit policy, and customer support that is slow or unhelpful. For an individual creator, that's annoying. For a business that relies on the platform for an important part of its operations, it's a serious risk.

## A great tool for creators, but not built for support teams

After digging through all the reviews and features, the takeaway is pretty clear. ElevenLabs is a fantastic, top-of-the-line tool for anyone who needs to generate realistic AI voices for content. Podcasters, YouTubers, audiobook producers, and developers who just need a best-in-class TTS engine will get a ton of value out of it. [...] ## Frequently asked questions

ElevenLabs excels at generating realistic voices, but according to reviews, it's not designed to be a complete customer support solution. It lacks the workflow automation, knowledge integration, and helpdesk connections necessary for a functional AI agent.

ElevenLabs reviews offer mixed feedback on voice cloning. While many find it impressive, some users report cloned voices can sound "horrificially fake" or artificial, highly dependent on the quality of the original audio input.

Many ElevenLabs reviews highlight frustration with the pricing model, particularly how quickly character credits can be consumed, especially with features like dubbing. Users also dislike that unused credits do not roll over.

results.title: ElevenLabs API Review: A Developer's Honest Take - YouTube
results.content:
The video dives deep into ElevenLabs as a case study for audio and video APIs, demonstrating how dubbing that would normally require dozens of manual steps can be reduced to a single command. Beyond content creation, the discussion explores practical applications for DevOps and SRE engineers: localizing documentation, generating voice alerts for incidents, auto-narrating demos, and more. The honest review covers both the impressive API quality and voice output alongside real frustrations like slow support, broken YouTube URL dubbing, and concerning terms of service around voice data rights. For engineers looking to add multimedia capabilities to their workflows, this offers a practical, code-first perspective on what works and what to watch out for.

#AI #SoftwareEngineering #ElevenLabs [...] support interaction can drag on and on and on for weeks. Next, dubbing from YouTube URLs does not work. It doesn't work. I spent time building code to call their API with YouTube links and it never ever ever worked. Not once. Now, support admitted it kind of. They say, "Hey, sometimes it doesn't work." But in my experience, it never does. It doesn't work. They should just remove that option from the API. And on top of that, uploading local files is limited to 1 GB. My videos are way bigger than that. So, I had to build additional logic to compress them before uploading. The API itself is great. It's amazing. But some of 11 Lab's underlying features have serious serious limitations. What else? Oh yeah, the terms of service are concerning. In February 2025, 11 Labs updated their terms of [...] auto narrate product demos from scripts and then you can add the audio description to internal tools screencast for accessibility or you can dub your I don't know cubecon presentation for international audiences. The point is multimedia APIs aren't just for content creators. There are tools that can solve real problems in engineering workflows. Now, 11 Labs isn't perfect. Let's be clear, no tool is. So, let's talk about what works well and what doesn't. Let me break down what works, what doesn't, and whether the tradeoffs are actually worth it. To begin with, support. Oh my god, it's so slow. It takes days to get a response. And when they do reply, it's usually a follow-up question that takes another few days to answer. A single support interaction can drag on and on and on for weeks.

results.title: The ElevenLabs problems
results.content:
Sitemap

Open in app

Sign up

Sign in

 it mistakenly reads presence tense. Or an all caps word is not read as it reads, but as an acronym (when it isn’t). These situations require correcting and re-rendering. The issue could be my fault or the fault of the service. Either way it can’t be re-rendered easily on the spot.

Any changes to the 5,000 characters already rendered will not re-generate, but will do a full new generation. So if it costs you 3,000 tokens to render, you have to do it each time to correct a mistake it made.

It’s so frustrating that I have the tokens to read a 30m podcast script, but after all the errors in reading and generating the fixes, that I only get 10m out of it and now my tokens are all consumed. [...] Most people will start off on the FREE plan with Elevenlabs. They like it, they create a voice. All is good. They just need more tokens, so they upgrade to the Starter plan, only to realize that the amount of tokens offered in this tier is too limited. This is where the problem comes in.

### Pricing Tier vs. Buying Tokens

Once you run out of tokens, you can’t just buy more tokens. Elevenlabs requires you to buy a bigger pricing tier. Unlike Google and other providers, ElevenLabs chooses not to sell token bundles. There’s no $20 token package. Instead they want you to upgrade the plan.

If you upgrade the plan to the “Creator” tier (which is only $22/mo)… you get more tokens, but if you hit the ceiling again, the next tier jumps to $99/month (the “Pro Tier”). [...] There’s a potential work around which involves copying out the paragraphs with errors, and doing a render of just that paragraph, then splicing the changed reading over the other output in post. It’s messy.

Now comes the issue of buying more tokens….

## The lack of linear pricing…

Perhaps the worst problem with ElevenLabs is their pricing model. I don’t know if they are locked into this because of a poorly designed architecture, or if it really is a business model, but it’s incredibly flawed.
